aggregateCoverage.label: Binding COP instruments
aggregateCoverage.detail: Five countries have enacted dedicated laws, regulations, or binding COP instruments.

aggregateCoverage.label: Draft pathways
aggregateCoverage.detail: Nigeria, Rwanda, and Zimbabwe have draft bill or policy pathways in motion.

aggregateCoverage.label: Strategy or guidelines only
aggregateCoverage.detail: Zambia, Malawi, and Lesotho have dedicated strategies or guidelines without primary legislative backing.

aggregateCoverage.label: General legal coverage
aggregateCoverage.detail: Thirty-nine countries still rely on broader cybercrime, penal, or data protection frameworks with only partial relevance.

aggregateCoverage.label: No identifiable framework
aggregateCoverage.detail: Eritrea, Libya, Gambia, Guinea-Bissau, and Burundi have no identifiable child online safety framework.

regionalReadiness.methodology: An AU COP Policy implementation scorecard should track legislative adoption, institutional capacity, enforcement activity, and child participation.
regionalReadiness.sourceNote: This regional view draws from the countries discussed in the analysis. No region-level readiness score is published.
